paragraph_text: The Beneficiary satisfies 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(viii) by demonstrating leading roles in distinguished organizations, including Peking University (ranked #17 worldwide) and through leadership of major commercial research projects for nationally prominent enterprises. [Exhibit A1, p.7; Exhibit E3, p.2; Exhibit E5, p.2; Exhibit E6, p.2; Exhibit E10, p.2] Peking University ("PKU") is among the most distinguished academic institutions in the world, having been "founded in 1898 as a replacement for the ancient Guozijian school (Imperial College)." [Exhibit A1, p.5] As the "oldest higher education institution in China," PKU played a pivotal role in the nation's intellectual history, serving as "a center for Chinese progressive thought" since the early 1920s. [Exhibit A1, p.5] PKU operates 30 colleges, 12 departments, and 93 undergraduate specialties, with 199 master's degree programs and "216 research institutes and research centres," along with "2 national engineering research centres" and "81 key national disciplines." [Exhibit A1, p.5] PKU was "China's first national comprehensive university" and has since "developed into a comprehensive university with outstanding achievements in the humanities, social sciences, and natural sciences." [Exhibit E1, p.2] The QS World University Rankings confirm that "Peking University is one of the top public universities in Beijing, China (Mainland)" and that "[i]t is ranked #17 in QS World University Rankings 2024." [Exhibit A1, p.7] The Beneficiary has been "employed by Peking University since Jul. 1992, and is working in School of Journalism & Communication as a professor." [Exhibit F1, p.2] The Beneficiary's total pre-tax salary from March 2021 to February 2022 was "¥961710.00 RMB," reflecting the premium compensation PKU provides to retain senior faculty of his caliber. [Exhibit F1, p.2] The Beneficiary holds the concurrent titles of "Professor" and "Vice Dean" at the School of New Media of Peking University, positions he has occupied since "Feb. 2014 to the present." [Exhibit E3, p.2; Exhibit E3, p.3] An official Peking University appointment document (X.F.[2022] No.13) confirms that "[t]he University has decided to appoint Gang Chen as the Dean of the School of Journalism & Communication at Peking University" and that "Dehuan Liu, Kaihe Chen (with a probation period of one year), and Weijia Wang (also with a probation period of one year) have been appointed as Vice Deans." [Exhibit E5, p.2] Notably, unlike the other Vice Deans, the Beneficiary was appointed without a probation period, reflecting the University's established confidence in his leadership abilities. [Exhibit E5, p.2] Beyond his Vice Dean position, the Beneficiary also serves as "Director" of the Academic Committee of the School of Journalism & Communication, as confirmed by the Secretariat of the Academic Committee of Peking University. [Exhibit E6, p.2] The Academic Committee membership under his directorship comprises: "Kaihe Chen (Vice Director), Weijia Wang, Wei Li, Jing Wu, Gang Chen, Shaoyang Lu, Jinnan Tang, Xinzhou Xie." [Exhibit E7, p.2] The organizational chart of the School further confirms the Beneficiary's membership on the Academic Degree Evaluation Committee Branch alongside "Shaoyang Lu (President), Wei Li (Vice President), Xinzhou Xie, Gang Chen, Di Lu, Dehuan Liu, Zengzhi Shi, Rudong Chen, Jing Wu, Yong Hu, Jing Xu, Kaihe Chen." [Exhibit E7, p.2] The Beneficiary also serves on the Appointment Committee of the School of Journalism & Communication, where the membership includes "Gang Chen (Director), Jinnan Tang, Liang Lu, Dehuan Liu, Kaihe Chen, Weijia Wang," thereby providing him a direct role in faculty hiring and promotion decisions. [Exhibit E7, p.2] The Beneficiary was instrumental in establishing the School of New Media at Peking University, which was "[f]ounded in Feb. 2014" as "a scientific research and teaching institution focusing on the innovation and development of new media" that "strives to become an internationally renowned and domestic leading think tank, talent pool and achievement database." [Exhibit E2, p.2] In addition to his administrative leadership, the Beneficiary established the Center for Market and Media Studies at PKU and serves as its Director. [Exhibit E3, p.3] Under the Beneficiary's mentorship, the School of New Media "has nurtured numerous talents in sociology, journalism, and communication" who "have gone on to become young elites in their respective academic circles." [Exhibit E4, p.6] Among the most notable of these mentees is Lingfei Wu, now a professor at the University of Pittsburgh, who "published a research paper titled 'Scientific Research Team Scale and Disruptive Innovation' as the cover paper in the February 21, 2019 issue of Nature." [Exhibit E4, p.6] The Beneficiary's research output at PKU includes the acclaimed trilogy on Chinese internet research: "The Future is Happening," "Subversion and Reorganization," and "The Future without Limits," which is described as "the most important achievement from research on Chinese internet." [Exhibit E4, p.5] Professor Bangchang Xie, Vice President of Fu Jen Catholic University, attests that the Beneficiary has actively contributed to the establishment of academic communities in the fields of sociology, journalism, and communication through his leadership at PKU. [Exhibit E2, p.2] The Beneficiary also served as the Project Principal for a major commercial research collaboration between Peking University and Mixuebingcheng (MXBC), a national beverage chain brand. [Exhibit E10, p.2; Exhibit E11, p.2] The formal contract identifies the Beneficiary as "Project Principal: Dehuan Liu" and specifies that the parties would cooperate on the project "Research on New Trends," initiated on October 20, 2021, with deliverables including "one monograph of Research on New Trends and four phased research reports." [Exhibit E10, p.2; Exhibit E10, p.3] The total service fee for the project in 2021 was RMB 610,000 (tax-inclusive), reflecting the substantial commercial value placed on the Beneficiary's research leadership. [Exhibit E10, p.4] Mixuebingcheng is a nationally prominent enterprise with total funding of CN¥2 billion, further demonstrating that the Beneficiary's research leadership was sought by organizations of significant stature in China's commercial landscape. [Exhibit E11, p.2] In sum, the foregoing evidence — spanning the Beneficiary's Vice Dean and Academic Committee Director positions at Peking University, his institutional development contributions including the establishment of the School of New Media and the Center for Market and Media Studies, and his leadership of major commercial research projects for enterprises such as Mixuebingcheng — clearly establishes a pattern of leading roles in distinguished organizations that places the Beneficiary among the small percentage of professionals who have risen to the very top of the field. [Exhibit A1, p.7; Exhibit E3, p.2; Exhibit E5, p.2; Exhibit E6, p.2; Exhibit E10, p.2]

sentences.text: The Beneficiary satisfies 8 C.F.R. §204.5(h)(3)(viii) by demonstrating leading roles in distinguished organizations, including Peking University (ranked #17 worldwide) and through leadership of major commercial research projects for nationally prominent enterprises. [Exhibit A1, p.7; Exhibit E3, p.2; Exhibit E5, p.2; Exhibit E6, p.2; Exhibit E10, p.2]

Peking University ("PKU") is among the most distinguished academic institutions in the world, having been "founded in 1898 as a replacement for the ancient Guozijian school (Imperial College)." [Exhibit A1, p.5]

As the "oldest higher education institution in China," PKU played a pivotal role in the nation's intellectual history, serving as "a center for Chinese progressive thought" since the early 1920s. [Exhibit A1, p.5]

PKU operates 30 colleges, 12 departments, and 93 undergraduate specialties, with 199 master's degree programs and "216 research institutes and research centres," along with "2 national engineering research centres" and "81 key national disciplines." [Exhibit A1, p.5]

PKU was "China's first national comprehensive university" and has since "developed into a comprehensive university with outstanding achievements in the humanities, social sciences, and natural sciences." [Exhibit E1, p.2]

The QS World University Rankings confirm that "Peking University is one of the top public universities in Beijing, China (Mainland)" and that "[i]t is ranked #17 in QS World University Rankings 2024." [Exhibit A1, p.7]
The Beneficiary has been "employed by Peking University since Jul. 1992, and is working in School of Journalism & Communication as a professor." [Exhibit F1, p.2]

The Beneficiary's total pre-tax salary from March 2021 to February 2022 was "¥961710.00 RMB," reflecting the premium compensation PKU provides to retain senior faculty of his caliber. [Exhibit F1, p.2]

The Beneficiary holds the concurrent titles of "Professor" and "Vice Dean" at the School of New Media of Peking University, positions he has occupied since "Feb. 2014 to the present." [Exhibit E3, p.2; Exhibit E3, p.3]

An official Peking University appointment document (X.F.[2022] No.13) confirms that "[t]he University has decided to appoint Gang Chen as the Dean of the School of Journalism & Communication at Peking University" and that "Dehuan Liu, Kaihe Chen (with a probation period of one year), and Weijia Wang (also with a probation period of one year) have been appointed as Vice Deans." [Exhibit E5, p.2]
Notably, unlike the other Vice Deans, the Beneficiary was appointed without a probation period, reflecting the University's established confidence in his leadership abilities. [Exhibit E5, p.2]

Beyond his Vice Dean position, the Beneficiary also serves as "Director" of the Academic Committee of the School of Journalism & Communication, as confirmed by the Secretariat of the Academic Committee of Peking University. [Exhibit E6, p.2]
The Academic Committee membership under his directorship comprises: "Kaihe Chen (Vice Director), Weijia Wang, Wei Li, Jing Wu, Gang Chen, Shaoyang Lu, Jinnan Tang, Xinzhou Xie." [Exhibit E7, p.2]

The organizational chart of the School further confirms the Beneficiary's membership on the Academic Degree Evaluation Committee Branch alongside "Shaoyang Lu (President), Wei Li (Vice President), Xinzhou Xie, Gang Chen, Di Lu, Dehuan Liu, Zengzhi Shi, Rudong Chen, Jing Wu, Yong Hu, Jing Xu, Kaihe Chen." [Exhibit E7, p.2]

The Beneficiary also serves on the Appointment Committee of the School of Journalism & Communication, where the membership includes "Gang Chen (Director), Jinnan Tang, Liang Lu, Dehuan Liu, Kaihe Chen, Weijia Wang," thereby providing him a direct role in faculty hiring and promotion decisions. [Exhibit E7, p.2]

The Beneficiary was instrumental in establishing the School of New Media at Peking University, which was "[f]ounded in Feb. 2014" as "a scientific research and teaching institution focusing on the innovation and development of new media" that "strives to become an internationally renowned and domestic leading think tank, talent pool and achievement database." [Exhibit E2, p.2]
In addition to his administrative leadership, the Beneficiary established the Center for Market and Media Studies at PKU and serves as its Director. [Exhibit E3, p.3]

Under the Beneficiary's mentorship, the School of New Media "has nurtured numerous talents in sociology, journalism, and communication" who "have gone on to become young elites in their respective academic circles." [Exhibit E4, p.6]

Among the most notable of these mentees is Lingfei Wu, now a professor at the University of Pittsburgh, who "published a research paper titled 'Scientific Research Team Scale and Disruptive Innovation' as the cover paper in the February 21, 2019 issue of Nature." [Exhibit E4, p.6]

The Beneficiary's research output at PKU includes the acclaimed trilogy on Chinese internet research: "The Future is Happening," "Subversion and Reorganization," and "The Future without Limits," which is described as "the most important achievement from research on Chinese internet." [Exhibit E4, p.5]

Professor Bangchang Xie, Vice President of Fu Jen Catholic University, attests that the Beneficiary has actively contributed to the establishment of academic communities in the fields of sociology, journalism, and communication through his leadership at PKU. [Exhibit E2, p.2]

The Beneficiary also served as the Project Principal for a major commercial research collaboration between Peking University and Mixuebingcheng (MXBC), a national beverage chain brand. [Exhibit E10, p.2; Exhibit E11, p.2]

The formal contract identifies the Beneficiary as "Project Principal: Dehuan Liu" and specifies that the parties would cooperate on the project "Research on New Trends," initiated on October 20, 2021, with deliverables including "one monograph of Research on New Trends and four phased research reports." [Exhibit E10, p.2; Exhibit E10, p.3]
The total service fee for the project in 2021 was RMB 610,000 (tax-inclusive), reflecting the substantial commercial value placed on the Beneficiary's research leadership. [Exhibit E10, p.4]

Mixuebingcheng is a nationally prominent enterprise with total funding of CN¥2 billion, further demonstrating that the Beneficiary's research leadership was sought by organizations of significant stature in China's commercial landscape. [Exhibit E11, p.2]

In sum, the foregoing evidence — spanning the Beneficiary's Vice Dean and Academic Committee Director positions at Peking University, his institutional development contributions including the establishment of the School of New Media and the Center for Market and Media Studies, and his leadership of major commercial research projects for enterprises such as Mixuebingcheng — clearly establishes a pattern of leading roles in distinguished organizations that places the Beneficiary among the small percentage of professionals who have risen to the very top of the field. [Exhibit A1, p.7; Exhibit E3, p.2; Exhibit E5, p.2; Exhibit E6, p.2; Exhibit E10, p.2]
